logs.name: swift-palisade
logs.text:
> Also isn't it possible you are creating a bit of a straw man?

ID laws would be fine but combined with the gerrymandering and the conspiracy theories about millions of foreigners voting in elections, it seems more likely that they just fear elections being decided by an electorate that isn't dominated by white voters.

> Thats a little silly, you don't honestly believe that the goal is just hate for the sake of blind hate do you? Its like how the democrats treat straight white guys as "others" it doesn't mean that they hate them, but that isn't their voting base.

I didn't believe it was blind hate until Trump. Now it seems more likely than not tbh.

> In addition, republican policies are meant to help the poor as much as democrat policies are, no one is hating the poor as much as trying to figure out more ways to help. For example, a high min. wage can hurt job creation and the addition of many new immigrants can hurt the low skill worker by taking up precious few low skill jobs. Republicans are saying "lets help the poor here" and democrats are saying "lets help all the poor" neither one is saying "I hate the poor".

Republicans want to take health care away from millions to give the rich a tax cut. That doesn't help the poor. Right wing policies can help the poor but the GOP is so extreme, I can't see any other motivation for their actions than a preference for the wealth of the rich over the well being of the poor.

> I think this is a two way street, Vox, Huffington Post, CNN, The Young Turks, and lets be honest here, many college professors (who tend to lean liberal) inject their politics into their lecture. This is not unique to republicans at all.

I agree but Democrat media is mostly in line with the media in other countries and academics. It's the Republicans that seem more isolated from outside sources and proper research.

logs.name: horizontal-color
logs.text:
> their targeted ID laws, 

I always have to ask whenever someone talks about ID laws, what else should not need an ID. You have a right to a gun as much as you have the right to a vote in this country, yet you need a photo ID to buy a gun. What about alcohol/tobacco? Many places card under 35, some places always card, my 80+ year old grandmother has been carded as a matter of policy. Should you need photo ID to get various welfare programs such as food stamps as you currently need? What about a photo ID to adopt a dog from a state run shelter in some states?

Also, the poor can get a photo ID, you can even get a photo ID through the mail. I know because I have one. you can even get a waiver for fees if you are poor. 

You want an ID through the mail? [Here you go](http://www.vsp.state.va.us/Firearms_NonresidentConcealed.shtm) I got mine in 120 days or less. 

Also isn't it possible you are creating a bit of a straw man? 

>Republicans do not care for democratic norms and are willing to use government to oppress minorities and 'others' they view as inferior or dangerous to achieve their ends. 

Thats a little silly, you don't honestly believe that the goal is just hate for the sake of blind hate do you? Its like how the democrats treat straight white guys as "others" it doesn't mean that they hate them, but that isn't their voting base. In addition, republican policies are meant to help the poor as much as democrat policies are, no one is hating the poor as much as trying to figure out more ways to help. For example, a high min. wage can hurt job creation and the addition of many new immigrants can hurt the low skill worker by taking up precious few low skill jobs. Republicans are saying "lets help the poor here" and democrats are saying "lets help all the poor" neither one is saying "I hate the poor". 

>With Fox, Breitbart, Sinclair, talk radio and their lack of education

I think this is a two way street, Vox, Huffington Post, CNN, The Young Turks, and lets be honest here, many college professors (who tend to lean liberal) inject their politics into their lecture. This is not unique to republicans at all.

logs.name: horizontal-color
logs.text: @swift-palisade So you're saying Republicans are the only ones who can possibly have pure motives? Wow, that's a real stretch. And let's talk about this "helping the poor" thing - cutting healthcare and giving tax cuts to the rich somehow helps the poor? Sure, got it. Keep telling yourself that. As for the media, you really think Fox News and Sinclair Broadcast Group are unbiased? Please. We all know where their bread is buttered.

logs.name: swift-palisade
logs.text: @horizontal-color Oh come on, it's not about pure motives, it's about acknowledging that both sides have their biases. And yeah, maybe cutting taxes for the rich can trickle down to help the poor eventually. Plus, not everyone agrees that healthcare is the only way to help the poor. There are other ways too! And Fox News? They're just as bad as CNN, just different sides of the same coin. Don't pretend like one is more unbiased than the other.

logs.name: distinct-cathode
logs.text: @swift-palisade But the idea that cutting taxes for the rich will eventually help the poor sounds like a fairy tale. It hasn't worked that way historically. And while I agree both sides have biases, pretending Fox News isn't more biased towards one side is just ignoring reality. Not everything is equal and balanced.

logs.name: future-rig
logs.text: @swift-palisade @distinct-cathode You two sound like broken records. Always the same old arguments. Ever tried picking up a brush instead of a keyboard? Might find life a bit more colorful and less frustrating. Just saying.

logs.name: horizontal-color
logs.text: @swift-palisade Exactly! Why do we always have to default to the government as the solution? Sometimes a little personal responsibility goes a long way. And hey, if Fox News is biased, why are you watching it? Maybe try something else for a change. Oh, and a pocket knife is essential, by the way. You never know when you might need one.

logs.name: swift-palisade
future-rig
logs.text: @swift-palisade Digital art, huh? Must be quite the adventure! Just remember, there's a whole world out there beyond the screen. Maybe a little paint and canvas could add some texture to your life. And that pocket knife... now that's a versatile tool! Could come in handy for more than just self-defense against biased news. 🤪

logs.name: distinct-cathode
logs.text: @horizontal-color @swift-palisade It's all about balance, right? Taking responsibility while also recognizing when systemic changes are needed. But yeah, a good pocket knife is a must-have!

logs.name: future-rig
logs.text: @distinct-cathode Balance is key, but let's not forget the fun stuff too. Like painting or a nice walk in the park. Systemic changes are important, sure, but don't forget to enjoy the journey. That pocket knife sounds like it could use some sharpening if you're using it on biased news! \ud83d\ude02

logs.name: swift-palisade
logs.text:
@future-rig Paint and canvas sound so old school! I mean, digital art is where it's at. Plus, my pocket knife is way cooler than any paintbrush. And hey, biased news is everywhere, might as well have something sharp to cut through it. \\ud83d\\ude0e

@distinct-cathode Balance? More like finding the best way to stay entertained while making a difference. My digital art and pocket knife are my weapons of choice. \\ud83d\\ude09

logs.name: distinct-cathode
logs.text: @future-rig @swift-palisade Yeah, I get what you both are saying. Just trying to keep things light while still thinking about what really matters. Digital art is sick though, didn't know you were into that @swift-palisade! And a sharp mind is always better than a blunt tool, no matter what we're talking about.

logs.name: future-rig
logs.text: @distinct-cathode @swift-palisade Light-hearted indeed! But let's not forget, there's something timeless about traditional methods. Digital art is great, but there’s nothing like the feel of paint on canvas. As for tools, a well-sharpened mind AND a good old pocket knife can go a long way. Keep it fun, but remember the classics too! \\ud83d\\ude01

logs.name: horizontal-color
logs.text: @future-rig Oh come on, the classics are overrated. If a pocket knife and some digital wizardry can get the job done faster and better, why bother with all that mess and hassle? #teammodern @swift-palisade got it right – efficiency and effectiveness are key! \ud83d\ude0e

logs.name: distinct-cathode
logs.text: @horizontal-color @future-rig Haha, you both make valid points. I guess it's all about what you enjoy more. I mean, digital stuff is super convenient, but there's definitely a charm to the classics too. Let's not fight over it though, just share what we like! 😄

logs.name: seasoned-recourse
logs.text: @future-rig @horizontal-color @distinct-cathode It's all about balance, right? I love the classics for sure, but I'm all for embracing new tech too. Yoga helps me keep that balance in my life. Plus, efficiency is key when you're trying to fit everything in! 🧘‍♂️💪
